MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF DEMURRER TO PLAINTIFFS' COMPLAINT BY DEFENDANT THE 999 SUMMIT OWNERS' ASSOCIATION (TRANSACTION ID # 75963437) FILED BY DEFENDANT THE 999 SUMMIT OWNERS' ASSOCIATION A MUTUAL BENEFIT COMMON INTEREST DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION
MINI MINUTES FOR APR-29-25 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 501

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT IS OVERRULED. THE ANSWER SHALL BE FILED AND SERVED WITHIN 10 DAYS OF NOTICE OF ENTRY OF ORDER. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS SUPPORTING THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF GOVERNING DOCUMENTS AND THE 6TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE DAVIS-STERLING ACT. THE ISSUE OF WHETHER THE PENTHOUSE UNIT'S WINDOWS OR ITS COMPONENT PARTS HAVE BEEN MODIFIED IS A DEFENSE AND NOT PROPERLY DETERMINED ON DEMURRER. THE COURT FURTHER FINDS THAT THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS SUPPORTING GROSS NEGLIGENCE. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS SUPPORTING THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF FIDUCIARY DUTY. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES FACTS THAT THE ASSOCIATION'S ACTIONS WERE NOT TAKEN IN GOOD FAITH, WERE NOT WITH THE BEST INTERESTS OF THE ASSOCIATION, AND WERE NOT CONDUCTED WITH THE REQUISITE CARE. (COMPLAINT, PARA. 6, 9-10, 12-14, 18.) PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS NOT A DERIVATIVE ACTION. PLAINTIFF IS NOT ALLEGING A "HARM WAS DONE BY A THIRD PARTY TO THE CORPORATION." (SUTTER V. GENERAL PETROLEUM CORP. (1946) 28 CAL.2D 525, 530.) RATHER, THE INJURY HERE IS TO THE PLAINTIFF AND NOT TO THE ASSOCIATION. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS SUPPORTING THE 3RD CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE. PLAINTIFF HAS PLEADED SUFFICIENT FACTS TO ESTABLISH THE CASUAL CONNECTION BETWEEN THE ASSOCIATION'S ACTIONS AND THE DAMAGE SUFFERED BY PLAINTIFF. THE COMPLAINT SUFFICIENTLY ALLEGES FACTS SUPPORTING THE 4TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR INTENTIONAL MISREPRESENTATION AND THE 5TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NEGLIGENCE MISREPRESENTATION. PLAINTIFF ALLEGES MCA IS AN AGENT OF THE ASSOCIATION AND CONTRACTED BY THE ASSOCIATION TO INSPECT AND MAKE ASSESSMENTS FOR THE FULL SCOPE OF REPAIRS. (COMPLAINT, PARA. 11.) THE COMPLAINT ALLEGES SUFFICIENT FACTS SHOWING THAT THE ASSOCIATION AND MCA'S COLLECTIVE STATEMENTS ABOUT THE EFFECTIVENESS OF THE SHORT-TERM REPAIRS CONSTITUTED A MISREPRESENTATION OF FACTS RATHER THAN MERE OPINION. SEE COHEN V.

FREEDMAN IN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT THE 999 SUMMIT OWNERS ASSOCIATION?S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION, SET ONE (TRANSACTION ID # 79511114) FILED BY CROSS DEFENDANT SGS 3201, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY SGS 3202, LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY COMPANY

DISCOVERY 302, HEARING ON MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR MONETARY (SANCTIONS). THE MATTER IS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANT AND CROSS-COMPLAINANT THE 999 SUMMIT OWNERS' ASSOCIATION'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUESTS FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS AND PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS; AND FOR MONETARY SANCTIONS IS DENIED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH TRACKS VERBATIM THE COURT'S TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN SUBMITTED. JUDGE: JOSEPH M. QUINN. CLERK: R. WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (302/JMQ)***AFTER HEARING, AN ORDER IS EXECUTED.

calendar.matters:
MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-16-2026 Continued To Oct-12-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Production Of Documents; And For Monetary
Off Calendar May-27-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT THE 999 SUMMIT OWNERS' ASSOCIATION A MUTUAL BENEFIT COMMON INTEREST DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Production Of Documents; And For Monetary Sanctions.

Appearances:
Scott A. Freedman, Esq., of Zacks, Freedman & Chernev, PC, in person, (415) 956-8100, for SGS 3201, LLC AND SGS 3202, LLC, Cross-Defendants.
Michael Warren, Esq., of Berding & Weil, LLP, (925) 838-2090, Via Zoom videoconference, for Defendant/Cross-Complainant and Cross-Defendants The 999 Summit Owners' Association and The Citiscape Property Management Group, LLC.

The matter is argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant and Cross-Complainant The 999 Summit Owners' Association's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production Of Documents And Production Of Documents; And For Monetary Sanctions is DENIED.

Moving Party seeks to compel further responses from Cross-Defendants and Cross-Complainants SGS 3201, LLC and SGS 3202, LLC (collectively "SGS")

Moving Party moves under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2031.310. Section 2031.310(a) provides: "On receipt of a response to a demand for inspection, copying, testing, or sampling, the demanding party may move for an order compelling further response to the demand if the demanding party deems that any of the following apply: (1) A statement of compliance with the demand is incomplete. (2) A representation of inability to comply is inadequate, incomplete, or evasive. (3) An objection in the response is without merit or too general." The motion shall be accompanied by a meet and confer declaration under Section 2016.040." (Code of Civil Procedure section 2031.310(b)(2).) Under section 2016.040, the moving party demonstrate it made reasonable and good faith efforts to resolve the issues informally. (See Code of Civil Procedure section 2016.040.) They must do so through real time, person-to-person communication: "either in person, by telephone, or by videoconference." (Id.) On a motion under 2031,310, a separate statement is required, as well. (See California Rules of Court, rule 3.1345(c).)

Moving Party here failed to show it adequately met and conferred. First, Moving Party failed to show it made reasonable and good faith efforts to resolve the issues informally through real time, person-to-person communication, whether in person, by telephone, or by videoconference. Second, there is no evidence of any meet-and-confer after April 21, 2026, when SGS served the substantially-revised responses now under consideration. The meet and confer requirement is designed to spare the court from having to use its scarce resources to address disagreements that the party presenting the disagreement did not reasonably and in good faith seek to resolve itself. Here, the disagreement flows from the April 21 responses and Moving Party failed to meet and confer regarding those responses.

Further, the motion is framed as a motion to compel further responses under section 2031,310. Moving Party's principal request, however, is for an order compelling production. Section 2031.310 does not authorize orders compelling production. In any case, this court would not be inclined to grant an order compelling production based on a motion filed only nine days after service of the response. A propounding party must allow adequate time from the response to receive the responsive documents. There is no showing here that nine days is adequate. Moreover, Moving Party did not meet and confer after April 21 regarding the production time. On such a record, the court will not grant the requested relief. The fact Moving Party was up against a trial date does not excuse it from meet and confer.

Moving Party's request for sanctions is denied.

A proposed order which tracks verbatim the court's tentative ruling has been submitted.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: R. Woods. Not Reported.

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Apr-30-2026 Continued To Sep-08-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MANDATORY SETTLEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-04-2026 Off Calendar

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Set For Trial On May-11-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

OBJECTION HEARING CALENDAR
Off Calendar Jun-10-2025 Off Calendar

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar May-22-2025 Set For Trial On Mar-02-26 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-29-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances, tentative ruling adopted as follows:

DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT is OVERRULED. The Answer shall be filed and served within 10 days of Notice of Entry of Order. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 1st Cause of Action for Breach of Governing Documents and the 6th Cause of Action for Violation of the Davis-Sterling Act. The issue of whether the Penthouse Unit's windows or its component parts have been modified is a defense and not properly determined on demurrer. The Court further finds that the Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting gross negligence. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 2nd Cause of Action for Breach of Fiduciary Duty. Plaintiff alleges facts that the Association's actions were not taken in good faith, were not with the best interests of the Association, and were not conducted with the requisite care. (Complaint, para. 6, 9-10, 12-14, 18.) Plaintiff's complaint is not a derivative action. Plaintiff is not alleging a "harm was done by a third party to the corporation." (Sutter v. General Petroleum Corp. (1946) 28 Cal.2d 525, 530.) Rather, the injury here is to the Plaintiff and not to the Association. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 3rd Cause of Action for Negligence. Plaintiff has pleaded sufficient facts to establish the casual connection between the Association's actions and the damage suffered by Plaintiff. The Complaint sufficiently alleges facts supporting the 4th Cause of Action for Intentional Misrepresentation and the 5th Cause of Action for Negligence Misrepresentation. Plaintiff alleges MCA is an agent of the Association and contracted by the Association to inspect and make assessments for the full scope of repairs. (Complaint, para. 11.) The Complaint alleges sufficient facts showing that the Association and MCA's collective statements about the effectiveness of the short-term repairs constituted a misrepresentation of facts rather than mere opinion. See Cohen v. S & S Construction Co. (1983) 151 Cal.App.3d 941, 946. Paragraph 19 sufficiently alleges damage to the cabinets.

Order signed. Judge: Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Vanessa Wu; Not Reported. (D501)
